name: Lee Morgan
abstract: Edward Lee Morgan (July 10, 1938 – February 19, 1972) was an American jazz trumpeter and composer. One of the key hard bop musicians of the 1960s and a cornerstone of the Blue Note label, Morgan came to prominence in his late teens, recording with bandleaders like John Coltrane, Curtis Fuller, Dizzy Gillespie, Hank Mobley, and Wayne Shor…

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions 'Lee Morgan' only as a label/name in the interviewer's prompt. The interviewee's response immediately following the mention discusses Larry Ridley, not Lee Morgan. There is no text where David N. Baker describes a personal or professional relationship with Lee Morgan.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I thought it was tragic how many of our great musicians like Bird, like Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and so many others man, you know Bud Powell, that had tragic life circumstances
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Cranshaw
about_quotes.evidence: Bob has performed with Lee Morgan, Wes Montgomery, Horace Silver, Ella Fitzgerald and Joe Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Cranshaw interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: in the next column is a picture of Lee Morgan. And the headline was “Lee Morgan skudt i New York.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a lot of Blue Note records, Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and Horace Silver...
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Mickey Roker
about_quotes.evidence: So finally one day after I’d been in New York for a while, I was working with Lee Morgan at the time... So I did that for a while and then I left there and that’s when I joined Lee Morgan’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mickey Roker interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995
